title: gilia gracilis var. congesta
intro: Gilia gracilis var. congesta, also known as Sticky Gilia or Purple Gilia, is a perennial plant native to the western United States, characterized by its vibrant purple flowers and sticky foliage. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds. Its medicinal properties have been linked to several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving congestion, and promoting wound healing. Gilia gracilis var. congesta can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in traditional herbal remedies to address various health concerns.
